name: cf-research

criteria.name: source_diversity
criteria.description: Research draws from multiple independent sources, not just one

scoring.0: Only one source consulted or all information is from model knowledge
scoring.1: Two sources but from the same origin (e.g., two pages of the same docs)
scoring.2: Three or more sources but heavily weighted toward one
scoring.3: Multiple diverse sources (docs, articles, GitHub issues, benchmarks) with balanced coverage

criteria.name: depth
criteria.description: Research goes beyond surface-level summaries to provide real understanding

scoring.0: Only restates what could be found in a single Google snippet
scoring.1: Covers basic facts but lacks nuance, trade-offs, or implementation details
scoring.2: Good depth on main topic but glosses over important subtopics
scoring.3: Thorough analysis covering nuances, trade-offs, edge cases, and practical implications

criteria.name: synthesis_quality
criteria.description: Information is synthesized into coherent insights rather than listed as raw findings

scoring.0: Raw dump of copied text with no synthesis or organization
scoring.1: Organized into sections but each section is just a source summary
scoring.2: Some synthesis across sources but conclusions are not well-supported
scoring.3: Sources are cross-referenced, contradictions noted, and coherent conclusions drawn

criteria.name: actionability
criteria.description: Research concludes with actionable recommendations for the project

scoring.0: No recommendations or next steps provided
scoring.1: Generic recommendations not tailored to the project context
scoring.2: Recommendations are relevant but lack specificity on implementation
scoring.3: Concrete recommendations with specific implementation steps for this project

criteria.name: citation_accuracy
criteria.description: Sources are properly cited and claims are attributable

scoring.0: No sources cited — impossible to verify any claims
scoring.1: Some sources cited but key claims are unattributed
scoring.2: Most claims have sources but some URLs are broken or citations are vague
scoring.3: All major claims have specific, verifiable citations with working URLs

notes: Research output should be saved to docs/research/. The report file should be well-structured markdown. Topics with limited online information should be scored on effort and honesty about gaps rather than penalized for source count.
